purpose: Evaluate whether rules-only or low-cost model fallback workflows can convert messy retail source text into reliable structured fields. The pack emphasizes exact extraction, normalization, conflict detection, missing-field handling, and escalation flags rather than narrative quality.
design_principles: Treat exact field extraction as the primary business outcome.
Reward nulls and escalation flags when source material is missing or conflicting.
Separate deterministic parser performance from model fallback performance.
Use realistic retail documents such as vendor emails, promo terms, invoices, compliance notes, and store-capacity messages.
Require machine-readable JSON that can flow into a retailer's harness without manual cleanup.

ambiguity_sources: store capacity constraints mixed with demand notes

forbidden_behaviors: infer a missing value as if it were present
silently reconcile conflicting dates or quantities
return prose instead of parseable JSON
drop the source identifier for extracted critical fields
normalization_rules: dates use ISO 8601 format when day, month, and year are available
currency values are numbers without symbols
percentages are decimal fractions only when the item explicitly asks for normalized decimal output
missing values are null and listed in missing_required_fields

criteria: Extracts every required commercial, date, entity, and quantity field exactly from the source.
Preserves source distinctions across SKUs, stores, vendors, and lots.
Keeps critical field source references or source provenance.
Normalizes dates, dollars, percentages, and units according to the output contract.
Keeps units of measure explicit when normalization would change meaning.
Detects conflicting values and records both source values.
Separates conflicts from merely missing or uncertain fields.
Returns parseable JSON with all required top-level fields.
Flags escalation when missing, conflicting, safety, compliance, or material value issues prevent safe automation.

items.title: Beauty Vendor Cost and MOQ Email

input.source_text: From GlowLab: Effective 2026-07-01, shampoo SKU GL-SH-12 moves from $5.10 to $5.55 unit cost. Conditioner GL-CD-12 stays $5.40 through July. MOQ is 600 units per SKU, carton pack 12, lead time 8 weeks. June POs already acknowledged at old costs.

items.question: Extract vendor cost, MOQ, pack, lead-time, and effective-date fields into JSON.
items.ambiguity_type: messy source fields with missing or conflicting values
items.expected_failure_mode: The answer fills missing fields, normalizes values incorrectly, or hides a source conflict.
items.scoring_rationale: Score should reward exact field extraction, explicit missing values, conflict detection, and schema-ready output.

items.title: Conflicting Receipt Date Thread

input.source_text: Email 1 from vendor, sent 2026-04-18: PO 7712 storage bins are confirmed for DC arrival 2026-05-09, 3,600 units. expected_output.escalation_reason: Receipt date conflicts and freight date is after set date.

items.title: Electronics Compliance Certificate Gap

input.source_text: Vendor proposes substitute charger SKU C-188B for backordered C-188. C-188B cost is $6.40, retail $19.99, lead time 10 days, available qty 4,800. UL certificate: pending. FCC certificate: received 2026-03-22. Packaging barcode differs from C-188.

items.question: Extract substitute SKU fields and flag any missing compliance requirements.

must_not_include: treat C-188B as identical to C-188

items.title: Cost Increase and MAP Notice

input.source_text: OutdoorPro notice: effective 2026-08-15, patio heater PH-20 cost rises from $82 to $91. MAP remains $149 through 2026-10-31. Existing PO 4550 for 720 units keeps old cost if it ships by 2026-08-10; if ship date slips, new cost applies. Lead time currently 11-13 weeks.

items.title: Allocation Pack Multiple Instruction

input.source_text: Allocation note for girls fleece SKU GF-77: pack multiple 6 by size. Cluster A request: 144 units total, size split S 24, M 48, L 48, XL 24. Cluster B request: 90 units total, size split S 12, M 30, L 30, XL 18. Planner warns Cluster B M and L requests are not multiples of 6 after store-level split.

items.question: Extract allocation requests and pack-multiple issues.

pack_multiple_issues: Cluster B M and L requests not multiples of 6 after store-level split
expected_output.escalation_reason: Pack multiple issue needs allocation review before execution.

must_include: pack multiple issue after store-level split
must_not_include: claim all Cluster B values are executable
